All right, so the year of change sale. So we made it to the sale. Basically what we wanna do here is we want to make sure that we first have the smaller commitment. So the 12 month has been locked in. So whether you have locked that in at the first initial orientation, like let's say you sign them up for a green play or something like that and you already have them committed to a 12 month membership, then you can use this at the halfway meeting or the abandoned hope. Most of us don't already have them on a 12 month membership. So then this sale would take place on way out. So if you close them at the 12 month membership at the halfway meeting at the abandoned hope, which you should have, whether that's exchanging their credit or however you got them to sign a 12 month contract, this would happen the next time, so on way out day. And they will show up to way out day because you have them in a 12 month membership. So the point of this sale is to now exchange that 12 month commitment to a paid in full. So then you can use that to acquire more clients. So basically that's what this step one goes through and that's what step two goes through. My point of putting this here is to have you not try to get step one and step two in the same meeting because it will be more difficult to bridge them from a non-member directly to a paid in full member. It could be done, but it's much easier to get them on that 12 month commitment, like on weekly or monthly payments, however you do it, and then have a meeting a few weeks later and then get them to exchange that. So here's how the sale would go. And remember, this is an in-person sale, so you'll want to memorize this. You don't want to have this in front of you. You could convert this over to a phone sale script, but it will probably reduce your chances of success because it's hard to get that kind of money over the phone. So let's use this as an in-person. So basically they come into your office or wherever you guys normally meet the chat and you'll say, hey, name, how's everything going so far? And they'll be like, hey, and it'll say first, I want to say congrats on the progress you've made with the six week challenge. So this is kind of worded so you can use it for a halfway meeting or for a way out, just as progress. So, and they'll say, awesome, thanks. They'll be like, okay, so it looks like you've lost X pounds and X body fat percentage. That's so cool. I bet you feel great, right? So pumping them up, getting them excited. Again, this kind of works for the halfway meeting or the way out. And they'll be like, yeah, I do, I feel great. And you'll say, awesome. So now what are the goals that you're ready to attack? And they'll say, they'll kind of explain what they want to do next. If they're like, oh, I don't know, I'll keep losing weight. Get them to be a little bit more specific. You know, get them to give you goals that are either lifestyle changes or that are going to take a little bit of time to accomplish those goals. And that's what you say, awesome. So if you stay consistent, you'll reach those goals without a doubt, especially if you're on the Euro change program. So here, let me explain that a little bit more, okay? So that's one way that we do it. We'll say, no problem, you could definitely reach those goals, especially if you're doing this program. And then you could say, like, here, let me explain that a little bit more. Or you can leave that part out and they'll be like, hey, what's that? It's even better if you can get them to ask what it is. That's even better. But we found that sometimes people don't ask what it is. And then we're like, well, let me explain it since you didn't ask. So I put that into the script where it says here, let me explain a little bit more because then at least you're pitching it to a hundred percent of the people. But if you're a great salesman and you're good at sales, try to get them to ask you what it is by referencing it. So that's when you break down what the program is. So the Euro change program, and you'll want to say this a little bit, like a real conversation, but basically like, hey, the Euro change program is something that we designed to help our members reach long-term goals within a year's timeframe. Okay, so in addition to the membership you've already signed up for, here's what you'll be getting. And you want to emphasize that, let them know, like, hey, just so you know, you're already committed to a long-term membership, but here's what I want to give you in addition to that. Our 42-day detox program, which is like another six-week challenge for free. And so the 42-day detox, maybe you sold them on the six-week challenge initially, or you can flip those two around. So you could say, I'm going to give you our six-week challenge program if you initially sold them on the 42-day detox, and they're in that portal and everything like that, just flip-flop the wording. And they'll immediately associate that with a ton of value, because they probably bought it for like 499, 599, something like that. Or so they'll be like, wow, that's awesome. I could hold another one of these for free. You know, I just bought this thing for 600 bucks. And then you'll also get a long-term goal setting and reaching an appointment with our accountability coach. So you'll set that up. A spot on the EuroChange clipboard by the scale where we can track your monthly results. And you'll be added to the EuroChange Facebook accountability group, where we'll have everyone else who set long-term goals can help keep each other accountable. And they'll be like, wow, that's amazing. How does that work? Oh, great that you asked. Yeah, it's really cool. And it's super simple to get signed up. Since you're already signed up for a 12-month membership with us, all you have to do is one, let me know that you want to enter the program, that you want to do it. And number two, that you're able to prepay for the next 10 months of your membership with us. And we'll give you the last two for free, right? So one month is for you making a commitment to us. And the other month is for you making a commitment to yourself, okay? So it's almost like changing your current rate of X per X to X per X, is that cool? And that's where you're gonna want to have the math in there. So like, my as an example would be like, so it's kind of like changing your current rate of $49 per week to $39 per week. Is that cool? And they'll be like, wow, that's really cool. How much would that be? So people like to know like the all in, like what's my all in cost? And it'll be like, wow, that's really cool. How much will that be? Well, 12 months was 12, you always want to say what their saving, what their large sum of savings is. So I would say 12 months with us is normally $2,500. But with this program, it's only $19.99. And I wrote in here, use your actual numbers. If you want, we can just add that to the card you have on file and get rid of your weekly payments. Sound like a plan? And then boom, if they say, yeah, be like, awesome, just sign this real quick, saying that we're exchanging your weekly payments for your savings and your yearly payment. So that's when you have them do the contract. And then I put some notes, have them do the contract and then immediately fulfill on everything that you just promised because that's going to reduce the chance of them backing out. So get them scheduled right away with that accountability coach appointment, which that can be even another sale. You could sell them on HTTP, you could sell them, but set them up with that accountability coach and make sure they understand the six week challenge or their 42 day detox that they're about to be doing. And that accountability coach like sets long term, the point is that the accountability coach sets 12 month goals with them, right? That's why they're doing it. And whether they break it up into phase one, two, three, four, by this month we want to hit this, by this month we want to do this, it's just a goal setting appointment and making sure they understand the new meal plan. So get that scheduled. Then make sure that you add them to that clipboard. So you should have a year of change clipboard in your, wherever you have your other clipboards. And it should just be blank spots. You go in there right away and you write their name on there and you write what their goal is. And then it has 12 spots where they can track progress towards that goal over the course of 12 months. Okay, so get their name on there. Make sure you add them to the Facebook group right away and make sure you add them to the members portal. So for the 42 day or the six week, whichever one that they didn't have, whichever one you're giving them, add them to that right away. Because as soon as they have all of that, the likelihood of them backing out and be like, you know what, I want to go back to weekly or whatever. It's like, dude, we already just gave you all this stuff. Like we fulfill, you know what I mean? So we found that they don't even ask because you've fulfilled your end of it, right? So make sure you do all that stuff. In the notes, I also put a reminder, don't go for the two sale, the 12 month and the paid in full, the 12 month commitment, and then also the paid in full in the same meeting, you might ruin that because if you don't get it, then it will be weird trying to sell it again in three weeks. Like they probably still will say no. I wrote in here, if you did get the 12 month commitment on initial orientation, so maybe you sign them up to Green Play and you do 12 months like right off the bat, then this would be awesome to pitch at the halfway point. And then again, make sure you do the math beforehand. So do your math beforehand. So like if your normal rate is $49 a week but they get a 599 credit applied, well that's 37.50 per week is what they would change to. So if you want to do the year of change, then you need to make sure the math is correct with what they're actually going to be paying and what they're actually going to be saving. So as an example, it might be, I think it's an additional, I think it's an additional 1500. Yeah, it would be additional. If they paid $500, if they get a $500 credit for, because they prepaid for the challenge or whatever, then instead of $2,000, they would owe you $1,500 because you're letting them use that credit. And so that's what your sale price would be. You're still getting two grand, they just gave you 500 before. Or whatever, whatever your real numbers are, just make sure you do the math beforehand. And then if you want to break it up, like and to show them what they'd be saving on a weekly monthly basis, apply that math to it as well and just know your numbers. So that's basically the sale. It's short and simple. You're giving them all this really cool stuff and you're giving them two months for free in exchange for their 12 month agreement. And the reason that we love this is because it's guaranteeing a lifetime value of $2,000 or 12 months, which is not guaranteed even if you have a contract that guarantees $2,400 over 12 months, not everybody fulfills on that, which is why we're gonna use this script to be able to sell them into a paid in full, we deliver and we crush it.
